bond_markets.summary: UK long-dated gilt yields in 2025–2026 have exceeded the peak levels seen during the 2022 Liz Truss mini-budget crisis, indicating higher structural borrowing costs.
bond_markets.implication: Government borrowing is more expensive, leading to fiscal tightening that shifts pressure onto households.

supermarket_prices.former_minister_testimony: A former minister reports that a £30 grocery basket now costs £90, even when relying heavily on reduced yellow-sticker items.
supermarket_prices.interpretation: Middle-class shock indicates deep structural inflation affecting all income levels.

high_street.description: The UK high street is described as a 'graveyard', reflecting widespread closures and economic hollowing.
high_street.implication: Local economic ecosystems have collapsed, reducing access to affordable goods and services.

welfare_reality.description: A skilled unemployed man on £400 per month Universal Credit faces impossible financial conditions.
welfare_reality.implication: Essential costs exceed income, creating chronic instability and preventing recovery.

systemic_correlation.summary: Bond market stability is being maintained by transferring instability onto households through high prices, reduced services, and inadequate welfare.
systemic_correlation.feeling: There is a widespread sense of betrayal that promised economic 'change' has not materialised.

Laos Cave Rescue – Xaysomboun Province – Jonathan Head (Bangkok WS)
Laos Cave Rescue – Xaysomboun Province (World Service / Jonathan Head, Bangkok)
description: Composite map/visual for the Laos cave rescue in Xaysomboun Province, linked to BBC World Service reporting by Jonathan Head (Bangkok). Used to explore overlap of gold geology, volcanic arc, and limestone cave systems.
notes: Image associated with analysis of reports that trapped villagers/miners entered a limestone cave system in Xaysomboun Province to look for gold, and with mapping of the Loei–Luang Prabang volcanic arc and karst–gold overlap.
